news.headline: Global LIMS market to grow by USD 1.62 billion (2024-2028) at 13.46% CAGR, driven by recent developments - Technavio.
news.summary: As of June 28, 2024, the global market for laboratory information management systems (LIMS) is projected to expand by approximately USD 1.62 billion between 2024 and 2028, indicating a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 13.46%. This data comes from Technavio's analysis, showcasing significant growth opportunities in the industry. The rise in...

news.headline: Fed inflation rate rises slowest since late 2020, investors hopeful for rate cuts; S&P 500 reverses.
news.summary: The Federal Reserve's crucial inflation rate showed its smallest increase since the end of 2020. This news led to optimism among investors regarding potential rate cuts, resulting in a rise in the S&P 500.

news.headline: A significant number of CEOs continue to back Biden over Trump, despite criticisms of his policies and rhetoric.
news.summary: Some corporate leaders express discontent with certain policies and rhetoric of President Biden, but a significant number have not collectively withdrawn their support.

news.headline: Cryptocurrencies remain stagnant despite US inflation drop; analysts puzzled. Find out why from Cointelegraph.
news.summary: Analysts and traders have long predicted that a decrease in inflation would support the crypto market, but prices have not seen a positive impact. Despite expectations, cryptocurrency prices remain low. Cointelegraph delves into the reasons behind this trend.

news.headline: Crypto prices on 6/28: BTC, ETH, BNB, SOL, XRP, DOGE, TON, ADA, AVAX, SHIB. Altcoins volatile, BTC dip viewed as a buy chance.
news.summary: Alternative cryptocurrencies have experienced a volatile week in trading, with Bitcoin price drops being perceived by traders as a chance to invest, evident from the increase in funds entering spot Bitcoin ETFs.

news.headline: Bitdeer secures lease in Ohio for mining site, gaining 570 MW power capacity for 30 years.
news.summary: Bitdeer has secured a 30-year leasing agreement with the Monroe County Port Authority for up to 570 megawatts of extra power capacity.

news.headline: Presidio Property Trust closes $1.74M public offering of 109,054 shares of Series D Preferred Stock.
news.summary: Presidio Property Trust, a diversified real estate investment trust, has recently completed a public offering of 109,054 shares of its 9.375% Series D Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Stock with a par value of $0.01 per share (NASDAQ: SQFT). The offering was priced at $16.00 per share, resulting in total gross proceeds of about $1.74 million, excluding underwriting discounts and offering expenses. This transaction took place on June 24, 2024, in San Diego.

news.headline: CertiK's dispute with Kraken highlights the importance of white hat hacking in cybersecurity, despite controversies.
news.summary: Performing ethical hacking, known as white hat hacking, is an essential aspect of cybersecurity. However, it can spark debates, as demonstrated in the recent incident involving CertiK and Kraken.

news.headline: AI news discusses artificial intelligence trends and top AI stocks to watch, beyond Nvidia. For more details, visit Investor's Business Daily.
news.summary: Discover the concept of artificial intelligence, a rapidly evolving technology. Explore the top-performing AI stocks to buy and monitor, focusing on industry leaders beyond Nvidia. Stay informed on the latest trends in artificial intelligence with insights from the Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Abacus Life closes public offering of 11.5M shares at $8.00/share, raising $92M gross proceeds, oversubscribed.
news.summary: Orlando-based company Abacus Life, Inc. (NASDAQ: ABL) recently completed a successful public offering, exceeding expectations by closing with 11,500,000 shares of common stock. This included an additional 1,500,000 shares purchased by underwriters at the public offering price of $8.00 per share. The total funds raised from the oversubscribed offering amounted to around $92 million before deducting underwriting discounts, commissions, and estimated expenses. Abacus is known for its focus on longevity and actuarial technology as a leading alternative asset manager.

news.headline: Fed's preferred inflation gauge, PCE index, expected to drop to a 38-month low of 2.6%, signaling potential for a market rally.
news.summary: Traders are getting ready for the upcoming release of the Personal Consumption Expenditure (PCE) price index data, which is considered the Federal Reserve’s preferred measure of inflation. Scheduled for Friday, the Bureau of Economic Analysis will also be releasing data on personal income and personal spending simultaneously. Investors are particularly interested in whether the Fed’s inflation measure will decrease as economists are currently predicting. A decline would reinforce confidence in efforts to steer price pressures towards the 2% target and heighten expectations for future interest rate cuts.According to estimates compiled by Econoday, the PCE price index is anticipated to slow down from 2.7% year-on-year in April to 2.6% year-on-year in May. The consensus range falls between 2.5% and 2.7%. On a monthly basis, the PCE price index is expected to rise at a slower pace of 0.1%, compared to the previous month's 0.3% increase. Furthermore, the core PCE price index is projected to ease from 2.8% to 2.6%. For more detailed information, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Cryptocurrency industry update: Jump Crypto CEO resigns, MiCA deadline approaching, and more corporate disputes.
news.summary: In this week's edition of Crypto Biz, significant developments include the resignation of the CEO at Jump Crypto, Animoca Brands re-entry into the public markets, and the ongoing corporate disagreement between Riot and Bitfarms. These updates, along with other news, showcase the dynamic landscape of the cryptocurrency industry.

news.headline: Desert Mountain Energy Corp. has granted corporate options on June 28, 2024, with tickers TSX.V: DME, U.S. OTC: DMEHF, and Frankfurt: QM01.
news.summary: Vancouver-based Desert Mountain Energy Corp. announced on June 28, 2024, that it has granted a significant number of shares. Trading under TSX.V: DME, U.S. OTC: DMEHF, and Frankfurt: QM01, the Company's President shared this development. Desert Mountain Energy Corp., commonly known as DME, made the announcement but refrained from revealing specific figures.

news.headline: Teamsters at Gardner Cryogenics secure substantial pay raises and end strike after ratifying first contract with 184 skilled workers.
news.summary: Teamsters Local 773 in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania successfully negotiated significant pay raises for 184 skilled workers at Gardner Cryogenics, bringing an end to a two-week strike. The workers have voted overwhelmingly to ratify their first Teamsters contract, marking a major victory for the union. This development comes after the strike that began on June 28, 2024, demonstrating the effectiveness of collective bargaining in securing improved wages and working conditions for employees.

news.headline: Global correction tapes market to grow by USD 117.1 million from 2024-2028, driven by increasing student demand.
news.summary: According to market research firm Technavio, the worldwide market for correction tapes is projected to increase by approximately USD 117.1 million between 2024 and 2028. The market is expected to experience a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 14.91% during this period. One of the main factors driving this growth is the rising demand for these products among students.

news.headline: Pembina Pipeline closes $950M note offering; $650M in Series 23 Notes with fixed coupon of 5.22%; positive news.
news.summary: Pembina Pipeline Corporation has successfully completed its Offering of $950 million in senior unsecured medium-term notes. The Offering, conducted in three tranches, included $650 million in senior unsecured medium-term notes, series 23 (the "Series 23 Notes"), with a fixed coupon rate of 5.22% per annum. This financial move is designed to strengthen Pembina's position in the market and support its business operations.

news.headline: Bill Clinton praises Biden's consistent leadership over the past three years. #Clinton #Biden #leadership
Aecon Group Inc. and Coastal GasLink Pipeline reached a settlement to resolve dispute over pipeline project in British Columbia.
news.summary: Today, Aecon Group Inc. (TSX: ARE) disclosed an agreement between SA Energy Group and Coastal GasLink Pipeline Limited Partnership regarding the construction of Sections 3 and 4 of the Coastal GasLink Pipeline Project in British Columbia. The settlement, announced on June 28, 2024, marks the resolution of the dispute between the parties. SA Energy Group, a partnership of Aecon Construction Group Inc. and Robert B. Somerville Co. Ltd., along with Coastal GasLink Pipeline Limited Partnership, have amicably settled their differences. This agreement represents a significant step forward and paves the way for the successful completion of the project.

news.headline: Lowercarbon & Crusoe host AI Hackathon in San Francisco with DOE & OpenAI for clean energy dev; 100+ participants.
news.summary: In San Francisco, Lowercarbon and Crusoe Energy Systems LLC disclosed plans for a "Hackathon for Clean Energy" hosted at Crusoe's offices. The event, starting Friday afternoon and spanning 24 hours, will include participation from OpenAI, offering credits, technical guidance, and involvement in the judging process. Experts from the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) will collaborate with around 100 engineers, designers, and product leaders to delve into the potential of artificial intelligence (AI).

news.headline: Western Forest Products Inc. (TSX: WEF) to release Q2 2024 results on July 31, 2024. Announcement made in Vancouver on June 28.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on June 28, 2024, Western Forest Products Inc. (TSX: WEF) announced that its financial and operating results for the second quarter of 2024 are scheduled for release on Wednesday, July 31, 2024.

news.summary: On June 28, 2024, Forsys Metals Corp. (TSX: FSY) (FSE: F2T) (NSX: FSY) shared positive news following their virtual annual shareholder meeting. news.headline: Bilibili Inc. (Nasdaq: BILI and HKEX: 9626) announces all Proposed Resolutions adopted at AGM in Shanghai.

The company is listed on Nasdaq (BILI) and HKEX (9626). This news was announced on June 28, 2024.

news.headline: PNC disclosed results of biennial stress test per Federal Reserve rules on June 28, 2024, following Dodd-Frank guidelines.
news.summary: In Pittsburgh on June 28, 2024, The PNC Financial Services Group, Inc. (NYSE: PNC) released the findings of its biennial internal stress test, as mandated by the Federal Reserve. The results of the test, designed to evaluate the company's resilience in adverse economic conditions, indicated that PNC Financial Services Group, Inc. is well-equipped to withstand financial stress. This assessment is crucial for maintaining the institution's stability and ensuring its ability to navigate challenging economic environments.

news.headline: SGH grants inducement awards to new CFO, Nate Olmstead, upon commencing employment on June 26, 2024.
news.summary: A technology company based in Milpitas, California, recently disclosed that they have granted inducement awards to their newly appointed Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, Nate Olmstead. These awards were given to Olmstead upon his joining the company on June 26, 2024.

news.headline: M&T Bank Corporation's preliminary stress capital buffer requirement is lowered to 3.8% from 4.0% by the Federal Reserve Board.
news.summary: In Buffalo, NY on June 28, 2024, M&T Bank Corporation revealed that the Federal Reserve Board has set the Company's initial stress capital buffer ("SCB") requirement at 3.8%, a decrease from the previous 4.0%. M&T Bank Corporation (NYSE: MTB) made this announcement today, signifying a favorable adjustment in their financial obligations per the Federal Reserve Board.

news.headline: Meijer doubles milk discount for SNAP participants, offering 40% off any brand/size of 1% or fat-free milk, expanding access to healthy foods.
news.summary: A retailer based in the Midwest, Meijer, has decided to enhance its initiative aimed at improving access to healthy foods. Effective June 28, 2024, the company is increasing its discount on milk for SNAP-eligible individuals, now offering a 40 percent discount on any brand or size of 1 percent or fat-free milk. This enhanced discount program seeks to support individuals in making healthier choices and focuses on increasing affordability of essential nutritious products.

news.headline: Positive inflation data may not suffice to boost Biden after debate, impacting his chances significantly.
Freshslice Pizza celebrates 25th anniversary on July 1, 2024. Offering 99 cent XL slice deal at all locations.
news.summary: In celebration of its 25th anniversary on July 1, 2024, Freshslice Pizza, a popular Canadian pizza chain based in Vancouver, is excited to announce special deals and promotions for its customers. On this significant day, Freshslice is offering its customers an amazing deal - an extra-large slice of pizza for only 99 cents at all of its locations. This exclusive in-store offer is a token of appreciation to the community for their support over the years.

news.headline: Santander Holdings USA, Inc. informed of 2024 stress capital buffer of 3.5% CET1, effective Oct 1, 2024.
news.summary: In Boston, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System recently notified Santander Holdings USA, Inc. (SHUSA) on June 26, 2024, regarding SHUSA’s new stress capital buffer (SCB) requirement. This requirement, set to take effect on October 1, 2024, will entail SHUSA maintaining an SCB of 3.5% of its Common Equity Tier 1 capital (CET1), leading to an overall CET1 capital requirement of 8.0%. The robust capitalization of SHUSA will support its planned capital actions in line with the updated SCB mandate.

news.headline: Biden aims to let Trump's tax cuts for the wealthy expire; Trump supports dropping the corporate tax rate to 21%.
news.summary: With the upcoming November election looming, President Joe Biden and former President Donald Trump have proposed contrasting tax policies for Americans once one of them assumes office in late January 2025. Trump plans to maintain the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act, encompassing a 21% corporate tax rate and reduced taxes for individuals, notably dropping the top earners' tax rate from 39.6% to 37%. This policy, set to lapse in 2025, significantly raised standard deductions from $14,600 to $29,200 for married filers according to FOX Business. Conversely, if Biden secures a second term, he intends to allow Trump's tax cuts benefiting the wealthy to expire while preserving cuts for those earning under $400,000.

news.headline: Supreme Court supports city ordinances on public camping, not cruel punishment. - Discovery Institute applauds ruling.
news.summary: In Seattle on June 28, 2024, the Discovery Institute commended the Supreme Court's decision in the case of Grants Pass v. Johnson, stating that city regulations banning public camping are not deemed as violating the Eighth Amendment's prohibition of "cruel and unusual punishment". The ruling in Grants Pass v. Johnson sets a precedent regarding the constitutionality of such ordinances.

news.headline: Igloo, Inc. acquires Frame Team to enhance Web3 brand-building, focusing on consumer blockchain. - MIAMI, June 28, 2024
news.summary: Igloo, Inc. has taken a significant stride towards establishing a fresh approach to building Web3 brands by acquiring Frame. As the parent company of Pudgy Penguins, Igloo aims to introduce the upcoming generation of builders and users to blockchain technology through community engagement. The acquisition of Frame marks a crucial development in Igloo's strategic vision for the future. This news was revealed in Miami on June 28, 2024.

news.headline: SC's Chevron ruling decreases fed agencies' authority. The 1984 decision to defer to agencies' interpretations of statutes is now limited.
news.summary: In 1984, a landmark decision established the principle that courts must defer to agencies' reasonable interpretations of unclear statutes. This ruling has influenced regulations across various sectors, such as healthcare, safety, and environmental protection.

news.headline: Vinci Partners acquires MAV Capital, expanding its alternative investment platform in Brazil. (Char. count: 91)

(NASDAQ: VINP), headquartered in Rio de Janeiro, revealed on June 28, 2024, that the company has successfully finalized a significant transaction. Vinci Partners is a well-known player in the industry and has established itself as a top performer in the Brazilian market. The specific details of the completed transaction, such as the financial figures and entities involved, were not disclosed in the announcement. BNY plans to raise quarterly common stock dividend by 12% to $0.47 per share, starting soon.

Aptiv to announce Q2 2024 results on Aug 1, 2024, with investor call at 8:00 a.m. EDT hosted by Kevin Clark and Joseph Massaro.
news.summary: Dublin-based company Aptiv PLC (NYSE: APTV) is set to announce its financial results for the second quarter of 2024 on August 1, 2024. The scheduled investor call will take place at 8:00 a.m. EDT on the same day. Aptiv's Chairman and CEO, Kevin Clark, along with Vice Chairman and CFO, Joseph Massaro, will host the call. Those interested can access the live webcast and presentation materials through the Aptiv Investor Relations website at ir.aptiv.com. Additionally, a replay of the call will be made available afterwards.

news.headline: Arrive AI's merger with Brüush (Nasdaq BRSH) hits snag, proposed triangular reverse merger scrapped, will not enter public market.
news.summary: Today on June 28, 2024, Indianapolis-based company Arrive AI revealed that its planned merger with Brüush (Nasdaq: BRSH) has hit a standstill. As a result, the proposed triangular reverse merger will not be the route through which Arrive AI will go public. The specific details regarding the next steps for Arrive AI were not disclosed.

news.headline: 40-year-old Supreme Court doctrine overturned, may benefit crypto firms. Legal experts optimistic.
news.summary: Legal experts have stated that the reversal of a long-standing Supreme Court doctrine, in place for 40 years, could empower struggling cryptocurrency businesses.
